solute.description: This molecule consists of a central benzene ring substituted with three functional groups: a primary amine, a phenolic hydroxyl, and a carboxylic acid. The carboxylic acid and hydroxyl groups are positioned ortho to one another, while the amine group is located para to the hydroxyl group (5-amino-2-hydroxybenzoic acid). This small, highly polar structure possesses significant hydrogen bonding capacity, acting as both a donor and acceptor through its multiple oxygen and nitrogen centers. The presence of both acidic (carboxyl/phenol) and basic (amine) moieties gives the molecule a distinct amphoteric character.

solvent.description: This molecule consists of a single central carbon atom covalently bonded to four chlorine atoms in a symmetrical tetrahedral arrangement. It is a fully halogenated methane derivative, specifically a perchlorocarbon, characterized by the complete replacement of hydrogen atoms with chlorine. Despite the high electronegativity of the chlorine substituents, the molecule is non-polar because its high degree of symmetry causes the individual bond dipoles to cancel out. It is a small, compact molecule that lacks any hydrogen-bond donating or accepting capabilities.
